texto: There arepeople everywhere. Lindsay wants to be sick, it's like he canfeelall theireyes on him, but he does it anyway and when he finally moves away agood minute later Valentine seems to have turned from himself into a sillybashful schoolgirl, blushing and smiling and not quite looking up."Oh," he says, like that explains everything."Yeah.""Thank you.""That's a shit thing to say when somebody's just ripped all hisprinciples in half to make you feel better.""Thank you very much?""You're welcome.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Richard Rider: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a educacao. O trecho “There arepeople everywhere. Lindsay wants to be sick, it's like he canfeelall theireyes on him, b…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que educacao, cute, lindsay-valentine pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de lindsay-valentine.

frase_original: There arepeople everywhere. Lindsay wants to be sick, it's like he canfeelall theireyes on him, but he does it anyway and when he finally moves away agood minute later Valentine seems to have turned from himself into a sillybashful schoolgirl, blushing and smiling and not quite looking up."Oh," he says, like that explains everything."Yeah.""Thank you.""That's a shit thing to say when somebody's just ripped all hisprinciples in half to make you feel better.""Thank you very much?""You're welcome.
